title: lolium temulentum subsp. arvense
intro: Lolium temulentum subsp. arvense, commonly known as false cleaver or wild oats, is a subspecies of Lolium temulentum, a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lolium temulentum subsp. arvense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Lolium temulentum subsp. arvense can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
